meta.tagline: The only AI glossary that teaches you connections, not just definitions

terms.name: RAG
terms.definition: Technique that retrieves relevant documents from a knowledge base and feeds them into an LLM prompt before generating a response.

terms.name: MCP
terms.definition: Anthropic's open protocol that lets LLMs connect to external tools, APIs, and data sources in a standardized way.

terms.name: API
terms.definition: A set of rules and endpoints that allow software applications to communicate with each other.

terms.name: Claude
terms.definition: Anthropic's family of large language models, known for safety alignment and long context windows.

terms.name: OpenRouter
terms.definition: A unified API gateway that routes requests to hundreds of LLMs from different providers through a single endpoint.

terms.name: Vercel
terms.definition: Deployment platform for frontend and full-stack apps with built-in AI SDK support and cron jobs.

terms.name: GitHub
terms.definition: Microsoft-owned platform for hosting code repositories with version control and collaboration features.

terms.name: OpenCode
terms.definition: Open-source AI coding agent that runs in the terminal and connects to multiple LLM providers.

terms.name: Vector
terms.definition: A numerical array representing the semantic meaning of text or data in high-dimensional space.

terms.name: Transformer
terms.definition: Neural network architecture based on self-attention, used by virtually all modern LLMs.

terms.name: Attention
terms.definition: Mechanism that lets a model focus on relevant parts of the input when generating each output token.

terms.name: Embedding
terms.definition: Dense vector representation of text, tokens, or data used for semantic similarity and retrieval.

terms.name: LLM
terms.definition: A massive transformer model trained on huge text corpora capable of understanding and generating human language.

terms.name: Fine-tuning
terms.definition: Further training a pre-trained model on a specific dataset to adapt it to a new task or domain.

terms.name: RLHF
terms.definition: Training technique where human raters score model outputs to guide the model toward better behavior.

terms.name: LoRA
terms.definition: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that trains small adapter matrices instead of all model weights.

terms.name: Quantization
terms.definition: Reducing model weight precision (e.g. 16-bit to 4-bit) to shrink model size and speed up inference.

terms.name: Token
terms.definition: Basic unit of text (subword or word) after tokenization; models process tokens as integer IDs.

terms.name: Tokenization
terms.definition: Process of splitting text into tokens (subwords or words) that a model can process numerically.

terms.name: Context Window
terms.definition: The maximum number of tokens an LLM can process in a single call, covering both input and output.

terms.name: Prompt
terms.definition: The text input you send to an LLM containing instructions, questions, context, and examples.

terms.name: Prompt Engineering
terms.definition: The practice of designing and optimizing prompts to reliably elicit desired behavior from LLMs.

terms.name: Few-shot
terms.definition: Providing a small number of examples in the prompt to help the model understand the desired format or task.

terms.name: Zero-shot
terms.definition: Asking an LLM to perform a task with no examples, relying purely on instruction.

terms.name: Chain-of-Thought
terms.definition: Prompting technique that instructs the model to reason step-by-step before giving a final answer.

terms.name: GPT
terms.definition: OpenAI's family of autoregressive language models; also the architecture style used by most modern LLMs.

terms.name: BERT
terms.definition: Early influential transformer model for language understanding tasks; bidirectional, not generative.

terms.name: Llama
terms.definition: Meta's family of open-weight large language models widely used for local and research deployments.

terms.name: Gemini
terms.definition: Google DeepMind's multimodal LLM family capable of processing text, images, audio, and video.

terms.name: ChatGPT
terms.definition: OpenAI's chat product powered by GPT models, one of the most widely used AI applications.

terms.name: Hallucination
terms.definition: When an LLM generates confident but factually incorrect or fabricated information.

terms.name: Vector Database
terms.definition: Database optimized for storing and querying high-dimensional vector embeddings for similarity search.

terms.name: Semantic Search
terms.definition: Search based on meaning rather than exact keywords, powered by vector embeddings.

terms.name: Pinecone
terms.definition: Managed vector database service optimized for fast similarity search at scale.

terms.name: Weaviate
terms.definition: Open-source vector database with GraphQL API and built-in ML model integration.

terms.name: FAISS
terms.definition: Meta's open-source library for efficient similarity search over dense vector embeddings.

terms.name: Supabase
terms.definition: Open-source Firebase alternative with Postgres, realtime, auth, and pgvector for AI applications.

terms.name: LangChain
terms.definition: Framework for building LLM-powered applications using chains, tools, memory, and agents.

terms.name: LlamaIndex
terms.definition: Framework focused on connecting LLMs to custom data sources for indexing and retrieval.

terms.name: Hugging Face
terms.definition: Platform hosting thousands of open-source models, datasets, and demo spaces for the AI community.

terms.name: GPU
terms.definition: Hardware accelerator with thousands of cores optimized for the parallel matrix math used in AI training and inference.

terms.name: TPU
terms.definition: Google's custom AI accelerator chip designed specifically for tensor operations in ML workloads.

terms.name: CUDA
terms.definition: NVIDIA's parallel computing platform and API used for almost all GPU-accelerated ML workloads.

terms.name: vLLM
terms.definition: High-throughput and memory-efficient inference engine for LLMs with PagedAttention.

terms.name: Docker
terms.definition: Containerization platform for packaging AI applications with all dependencies for consistent deployment.

terms.name: Kubernetes
terms.definition: Container orchestration system for automating deployment and scaling of AI inference workloads.

terms.name: Next.js
terms.definition: React framework by Vercel with SSR, API routes, and cron support; popular for AI web apps.

terms.name: Anthropic
terms.definition: AI safety company and creator of Claude, focused on building reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

terms.name: OpenAI
terms.definition: AI research company behind GPT, ChatGPT, DALL-E, and Whisper; offers a widely used API.

terms.name: Agent
terms.definition: An AI system that autonomously plans, uses tools, and takes multi-step actions to complete a goal.

terms.name: MoE
terms.definition: Architecture where each input token is routed to a subset of expert subnetworks, scaling capacity without full compute.

terms.name: KV Cache
terms.definition: Cache of past key/value states in transformer decoding to avoid recomputing previous tokens.

terms.name: Speculative Decoding
terms.definition: Technique where a small draft model generates tokens verified by a larger model to speed up inference.

terms.name: Multimodal
terms.definition: AI that processes and generates multiple data types including text, images, audio, and video.

terms.name: Constitutional AI
terms.definition: Anthropic's training method where the model critiques its own outputs against a set of principles.

terms.name: AI Safety
terms.definition: Research field focused on ensuring AI systems behave as intended and don't cause unintended harm.

terms.name: Alignment
terms.definition: Ensuring AI system goals and behaviors match human values and intentions.

terms.name: Explainable AI
terms.definition: AI systems and techniques that provide human-understandable explanations for their decisions.

terms.name: Diffusion Model
terms.definition: Generative model that learns to reverse a noise process, used in image and audio generation.

terms.name: GAN
terms.definition: Two-network architecture where a generator creates data and a discriminator judges its authenticity.

terms.name: VAE
terms.definition: Generative model that learns a compressed latent representation and can sample new data from it.

terms.name: Autoencoder
terms.definition: Neural network trained to compress data into a latent space and reconstruct it.

terms.name: CNN
terms.definition: Neural network architecture designed for grid-structured data like images using convolution operations.

terms.name: RNN
terms.definition: Neural network with loops that process sequences by maintaining hidden state across time steps.

terms.name: LSTM
terms.definition: RNN variant with gating mechanisms to better learn long-range dependencies in sequences.

terms.name: Gradient Descent
terms.definition: Optimization algorithm that adjusts model parameters in the direction of steepest loss reduction.

terms.name: Backpropagation
terms.definition: Algorithm for computing gradients through a neural network by propagating errors backward.

terms.name: Optimizer
terms.definition: Algorithm that updates model weights using gradients; common ones are Adam and SGD.

terms.name: Loss Function
terms.definition: Function measuring how far model predictions are from ground truth; minimized during training.

terms.name: Overfitting
terms.definition: Model performs well on training data but fails to generalize to new unseen data.

terms.name: Underfitting
terms.definition: Model is too simple to capture underlying patterns in training data.

terms.name: Dropout
terms.definition: Regularization technique that randomly deactivates neurons during training to prevent overfitting.

terms.name: Batch Normalization
terms.definition: Technique normalizing layer inputs across a mini-batch to stabilize and speed up training.

terms.name: Transfer Learning
terms.definition: Using a model pre-trained on one task as a starting point for a different but related task.

terms.name: Pre-training
terms.definition: Initial large-scale training of a model on a broad corpus before task-specific fine-tuning.

terms.name: Federated Learning
terms.definition: Distributed training approach where models are trained locally on devices without sharing raw data.

terms.name: Self-supervised Learning
terms.definition: Training paradigm where labels are derived from the data itself, no human annotation needed.

terms.name: Contrastive Learning
terms.definition: Training approach that pulls similar examples together and pushes dissimilar ones apart in embedding space.

terms.name: CLIP
terms.definition: OpenAI model trained to align images and text in a shared embedding space via contrastive learning.

terms.name: Stable Diffusion
terms.definition: Open-source text-to-image diffusion model by Stability AI capable of generating photorealistic images.

terms.name: DALL-E
terms.definition: OpenAI's text-to-image generation model that creates images from natural language descriptions.

terms.name: Whisper
terms.definition: OpenAI's open-source automatic speech recognition model trained on 680,000 hours of audio.

terms.name: Ollama
terms.definition: Tool for running open-source LLMs like Llama and Mistral locally on your machine.

terms.name: Mistral
terms.definition: French AI company offering efficient open-weight models known for strong performance at smaller sizes.

terms.name: Groq
terms.definition: AI inference company with custom LPU chips delivering extremely fast token generation speeds.

terms.name: Replicate
terms.definition: Platform to run machine learning models via API without managing your own GPU infrastructure.

terms.name: Modal
terms.definition: Serverless cloud platform for running AI and data workloads with fast cold starts.

terms.name: Gradio
terms.definition: Python library for quickly building shareable web demos of ML models with minimal code.

terms.name: Streamlit
terms.definition: Python framework for building data and ML web apps rapidly without frontend knowledge.

terms.name: MLflow
terms.definition: Open-source platform for managing the ML lifecycle including experiment tracking, model registry, and deployment.

terms.name: W&B
terms.definition: MLOps platform for experiment tracking, model versioning, and dataset management.

terms.name: MLOps
terms.definition: Set of practices combining ML and DevOps for reliable and scalable deployment of ML systems.

terms.name: CI/CD
terms.definition: Automated pipeline for building, testing, and deploying code changes reliably and frequently.

terms.name: GNN
terms.definition: Neural network designed to operate on graph-structured data by passing messages between nodes.

terms.name: Knowledge Graph
terms.definition: Graph database of interconnected entities and relationships used for structured knowledge representation.

terms.name: Neo4j
terms.definition: Leading graph database platform for storing and querying highly connected data.

terms.name: PostgreSQL
terms.definition: Advanced open-source relational database with pgvector extension for AI vector storage.

terms.name: Redis
terms.definition: In-memory data structure store used for caching, session management, and fast data retrieval in AI apps.

terms.name: Elasticsearch
terms.definition: Distributed search engine supporting full-text, vector, and hybrid search for AI applications.

terms.name: Twilio
terms.definition: Cloud communications platform providing APIs for SMS, voice calls, and WhatsApp messaging.

terms.name: ElevenLabs
terms.definition: AI voice synthesis platform offering realistic text-to-speech and voice cloning capabilities.

terms.name: N8N
terms.definition: Open-source workflow automation platform for building AI pipelines and integrating multiple services.

terms.name: Workflow Automation
terms.definition: Using software to automate repetitive tasks and orchestrate multi-step processes without manual intervention.

terms.name: FastAPI
terms.definition: Modern Python web framework for building high-performance APIs with automatic OpenAPI documentation.

terms.name: Multi-Agent
terms.definition: Systems where multiple AI agents collaborate, delegate tasks, and communicate to achieve complex goals.

terms.name: Orchestration
terms.definition: Coordinating multiple AI agents, tools, or services to work together in a structured pipeline.

terms.name: Tool Use
terms.definition: LLM capability to call external functions, APIs, or tools as part of generating a response.

terms.name: Grounding
terms.definition: Connecting LLM outputs to verified external information to reduce hallucinations.

terms.name: System Prompt
terms.definition: Instructions provided to an LLM before the user conversation to set behavior, persona, and constraints.

terms.name: Temperature
terms.definition: Sampling parameter controlling output randomness; higher values produce more creative responses.

terms.name: Top-k Sampling
terms.definition: Decoding strategy that samples only from the k most likely next tokens.

terms.name: Top-p Sampling
terms.definition: Decoding strategy sampling from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds p.

terms.name: Beam Search
terms.definition: Decoding strategy maintaining multiple candidate sequences and selecting the highest probability one.

terms.name: Perplexity
terms.definition: Metric for language models measuring how surprised the model is by the next token; lower is better.

terms.name: Benchmark
terms.definition: Standard dataset and metric for comparing model performance, e.g. MMLU, GLUE, HumanEval.

terms.name: MMLU
terms.definition: Widely used benchmark testing LLM knowledge across 57 academic subjects.

terms.name: HumanEval
terms.definition: OpenAI benchmark measuring LLM coding ability through Python programming problems.

terms.name: PEFT
terms.definition: Methods for fine-tuning LLMs by only updating a small subset of parameters.

terms.name: QLoRA
terms.definition: Combines quantization and LoRA to fine-tune large models on consumer GPUs with low memory.

terms.name: In-Context Learning
terms.definition: LLM's ability to adapt to new tasks using examples provided in the prompt without weight updates.

terms.name: Emergent Abilities
terms.definition: Capabilities that appear in large models not present in smaller ones, not directly trained for.

terms.name: Scaling Laws
terms.definition: Empirical relationships describing how model performance improves with more parameters, data, and compute.

terms.name: Inference
terms.definition: Using a trained model to generate predictions or outputs on new input data.

terms.name: Training
terms.definition: Process of adjusting model parameters on data to minimize the loss function.

terms.name: Deployment
terms.definition: Process of making a trained model available for production use via an API or application.

terms.name: Edge AI
terms.definition: Running AI models on edge devices like phones, IoT sensors, or edge servers rather than the cloud.

terms.name: SLM
terms.definition: Smaller, more efficient language models designed to run on limited compute, including edge devices.

terms.name: Phi
terms.definition: Microsoft's family of small but highly capable language models trained on high-quality data.

terms.name: Copilot
terms.definition: AI code completion tool by GitHub and OpenAI integrated into editors and IDEs.

terms.name: Cursor
terms.definition: AI-first code editor with deep LLM integration for editing, refactoring, and explaining code.

terms.name: VS Code
terms.definition: Microsoft's popular open-source code editor with extensive AI extension support.

terms.name: Code Generation
terms.definition: LLM capability to write, complete, or transform source code in various programming languages.

terms.name: CodeLlama
terms.definition: Meta's code-specialized LLM fine-tuned from Llama for code generation and completion tasks.

terms.name: Reasoning
terms.definition: LLMs that perform extended internal chain-of-thought before producing an answer, improving accuracy on hard problems.

terms.name: o1
terms.definition: OpenAI's reasoning model that thinks step-by-step internally before responding, excelling at math and science.

terms.name: DeepSeek
terms.definition: Chinese AI company offering high-performance open-weight LLMs at low cost, disrupting the AI market.

terms.name: Grok
terms.definition: Elon Musk's xAI company LLM integrated with X/Twitter data for real-time knowledge.

terms.name: Perplexity AI
terms.definition: AI-powered search engine that synthesizes answers with citations from the web in real time.

terms.name: Cohere
terms.definition: Enterprise AI platform offering language models, embeddings, and reranking APIs for business use.

terms.name: Reranking
terms.definition: Post-retrieval step that re-scores retrieved documents by relevance before passing them to an LLM.

terms.name: Chunking
terms.definition: Splitting large documents into smaller segments before embedding for effective RAG retrieval.

terms.name: Data Pipeline
terms.definition: Automated workflow that moves, transforms, and loads data between systems.

terms.name: ETL
terms.definition: Process of extracting data from sources, transforming it, and loading into a destination system.

terms.name: Feature Store
terms.definition: Centralized repository for storing and serving ML features consistently across training and inference.

terms.name: Model Monitoring
terms.definition: Tracking model performance, data drift, and prediction quality in production over time.

terms.name: Drift
terms.definition: Change in data distributions or model behavior over time that degrades production performance.

terms.name: A/B Testing
terms.definition: Controlled experiment comparing two model versions in production by splitting user traffic.

terms.name: WebSocket
terms.definition: Protocol enabling full-duplex communication between client and server for real-time AI streaming.

terms.name: Streaming
terms.definition: Sending LLM output tokens to the client as they are generated rather than waiting for completion.

terms.name: Rate Limiting
terms.definition: Restricting the number of API requests a client can make in a time period to prevent abuse.

terms.name: Webhook
terms.definition: HTTP callback triggered by an event in one service to notify another service in real time.

terms.name: GraphQL
terms.definition: Query language for APIs allowing clients to request exactly the data they need.

terms.name: REST API
terms.definition: API following REST architectural constraints using HTTP methods for CRUD operations.

terms.name: SDK
terms.definition: Collection of tools, libraries, and documentation for building applications on a platform.

terms.name: Vercel AI SDK
terms.definition: TypeScript toolkit for building streaming AI chat interfaces and RAG features in web apps.

terms.name: Self-Consistency
terms.definition: Prompting technique generating multiple reasoning paths and selecting the most common answer.

terms.name: Autonomous Agents
terms.definition: AI systems that operate independently, plan multi-step actions, and use tools to complete long-horizon goals.

terms.name: Memory
terms.definition: Mechanism allowing agents to store and retrieve information across conversation turns or sessions.

terms.name: Cron Job
terms.definition: Scheduled task that runs automatically at defined time intervals on a server.

terms.name: Realtime
terms.definition: Technology allowing clients to receive live data updates from a server as events occur.

terms.name: pgvector
terms.definition: PostgreSQL extension adding vector similarity search capabilities for AI embedding storage.

terms.name: Astro
terms.definition: Modern web framework for building content-focused sites with minimal JavaScript and island architecture.

terms.name: React
terms.definition: JavaScript UI library for building component-based web interfaces, widely used in AI app frontends.

terms.name: D3.js
terms.definition: JavaScript library for producing dynamic, interactive data visualizations using web standards.

terms.name: Force Graph
terms.definition: Graph layout algorithm using physics simulation to position nodes based on attraction and repulsion.

terms.name: Obsidian
terms.definition: Local-first note-taking app with a graph view showing connections between notes and concepts.

terms.name: Second Brain
terms.definition: Personal knowledge management system for capturing, organizing, and connecting information externally.

terms.name: Vision-Language Model
terms.definition: Multimodal model combining vision encoders and language models to process images and text together.

terms.name: GPT-4V
terms.definition: OpenAI's multimodal version of GPT-4 capable of understanding and reasoning about images.

terms.name: YOLO
terms.definition: Real-time object detection architecture that processes an entire image in one forward pass.

terms.name: Computer Vision
terms.definition: AI field focused on enabling machines to interpret and understand visual information from images and video.

terms.name: Object Detection
terms.definition: Computer vision task of identifying and locating objects within images by bounding boxes and labels.

terms.name: NLP
terms.definition: AI field focused on enabling machines to understand, generate, and manipulate human language.

terms.name: Sentiment Analysis
terms.definition: NLP task of identifying and categorizing emotional tone in text as positive, negative, or neutral.

terms.name: Text Classification
terms.definition: NLP task of assigning predefined categories to text documents.

terms.name: Named Entity Recognition
terms.definition: NLP task of locating and classifying named entities in text such as people, places, and organizations.

terms.name: Machine Translation
terms.definition: Automatically translating text from one language to another using AI models.

terms.name: Speech Recognition
terms.definition: Converting spoken audio into text using AI models.

terms.name: Voice AI
terms.definition: AI systems for processing, generating, and interacting through spoken audio.

terms.name: Text-to-Speech
terms.definition: Converting written text into spoken audio using AI voice synthesis models.

terms.name: Recommendation System
terms.definition: AI system that suggests relevant items to users based on their preferences and behavior.

terms.name: Collaborative Filtering
terms.definition: Recommendation technique that predicts preferences based on the behavior of similar users.

terms.name: Autonomous Vehicles
terms.definition: Self-driving cars and vehicles that use AI for perception, decision-making, and control.

terms.name: Reinforcement Learning
terms.definition: Training paradigm where an agent learns by interacting with an environment and receiving reward signals.

terms.name: Q-learning
terms.definition: Model-free reinforcement learning algorithm that learns optimal action-value functions.

terms.name: DQN
terms.definition: Reinforcement learning algorithm combining Q-learning with deep neural networks.

terms.name: PPO
terms.definition: Popular policy gradient reinforcement learning algorithm used in training LLMs with RLHF.

terms.name: Markov Decision Process
terms.definition: Mathematical framework for modeling decision-making in environments with stochastic outcomes.

terms.name: Neural Architecture Search
terms.definition: Automated method for finding optimal neural network architectures for a given task.

terms.name: AutoML
terms.definition: Automated machine learning that handles model selection, hyperparameter tuning, and pipeline design.

terms.name: Hyperparameter Tuning
terms.definition: Process of finding optimal configuration values for training algorithms not learned from data.

terms.name: Learning Rate
terms.definition: Step size controlling how much model weights are adjusted in each training update.

terms.name: Batch Size
terms.definition: Number of training examples processed in one forward and backward pass before a weight update.

terms.name: Epoch
terms.definition: One complete pass through the entire training dataset during model training.

terms.name: Checkpoint
terms.definition: Snapshot of model weights and optimizer state saved during training for recovery or evaluation.

terms.name: Model Registry
terms.definition: System for storing, versioning, and managing trained ML models throughout their lifecycle.

terms.name: Experiment Tracking
terms.definition: Logging parameters, metrics, code, and artifacts across training runs for comparison.

terms.name: Distributed Training
terms.definition: Training ML models across multiple GPUs or machines to handle large models and datasets.

terms.name: Data Parallelism
terms.definition: Splitting training data across multiple devices, each with a full model copy.

terms.name: Model Parallelism
terms.definition: Splitting a model across multiple devices when it's too large to fit on one.

terms.name: Privacy-Preserving ML
terms.definition: Techniques for training and using ML models without exposing individual's sensitive data.

terms.name: Differential Privacy
terms.definition: Mathematical framework for adding calibrated noise to data or queries to protect individual privacy.

terms.name: Data Privacy
terms.definition: Protecting personal and sensitive data from unauthorized access or misuse in AI systems.

terms.name: GDPR
terms.definition: EU regulation governing data protection and privacy affecting AI systems processing EU citizen data.

terms.name: Ethical AI
terms.definition: Development and deployment of AI systems following ethical principles of fairness, transparency, and accountability.

terms.name: Bias Mitigation
terms.definition: Techniques for reducing unfair systematic biases in training data or model predictions.

terms.name: Algorithmic Bias
terms.definition: Systematic unfair outcomes in AI systems arising from biased training data or model design.

terms.name: Fairness in AI
terms.definition: Ensuring AI systems produce equitable outcomes across different demographic groups.

terms.name: Black Box Problem
terms.definition: Lack of transparency in how AI models arrive at their decisions or predictions.

terms.name: Interpretability
terms.definition: The degree to which humans can understand and explain how an AI model makes its decisions.

terms.name: SHAP
terms.definition: Shapley Additive Explanations — method for explaining individual model predictions by feature contributions.

terms.name: LIME
terms.definition: Technique explaining individual predictions by approximating the model locally with an interpretable one.

terms.name: AGI
terms.definition: Hypothetical AI with human-level or beyond intelligence across all cognitive domains.

terms.name: Superintelligence
terms.definition: Hypothetical AI surpassing human intelligence in all domains, a major long-term AI safety concern.

terms.name: Neuromorphic Computing
terms.definition: Computing architectures inspired by biological neural networks, offering energy-efficient AI processing.

terms.name: Quantum AI
terms.definition: Applying quantum computing principles to accelerate AI algorithms and machine learning.

terms.name: Digital Twin
terms.definition: Virtual replica of a physical system that uses AI to simulate, monitor, and optimize the real-world counterpart.

terms.name: IoT
terms.definition: Network of physical devices embedded with sensors and connectivity that can collect and exchange data.

terms.name: Swarm Intelligence
terms.definition: Collective intelligence emerging from decentralized, self-organized multi-agent systems.

terms.name: Evolutionary Algorithm
terms.definition: Optimization algorithm inspired by biological evolution using selection, mutation, and crossover.

terms.name: BPE
terms.definition: Tokenization algorithm that iteratively merges the most frequent character pairs to build a vocabulary.

terms.name: SentencePiece
terms.definition: Language-independent tokenization library used by many LLMs including Llama and T5.

terms.name: T5
terms.definition: Google's transformer model framing all NLP tasks as text-to-text conversion.

terms.name: RoBERTa
terms.definition: Facebook's robustly optimized BERT pretraining approach with improved training methodology.

terms.name: Vertex AI
terms.definition: Google Cloud's unified ML platform for building, deploying, and scaling AI models and LLMs.

terms.name: Azure AI
terms.definition: Microsoft's enterprise AI services platform including access to OpenAI models via Azure.

terms.name: AWS Bedrock
terms.definition: AWS managed service providing access to foundation models from multiple providers via a single API.

terms.name: Cloud AI
terms.definition: AI services and infrastructure hosted on cloud platforms like AWS, GCP, and Azure.

terms.name: Serverless AI
terms.definition: Running AI workloads without managing server infrastructure, paying only for compute used.

terms.name: Load Balancing
terms.definition: Distributing incoming requests across multiple servers or model replicas to ensure reliability.

terms.name: Scalable AI
terms.definition: AI architectures and infrastructure designed to handle increasing load without degradation.

terms.name: Knowledge Distillation
terms.definition: Compressing a large teacher model's knowledge into a smaller student model.

terms.name: Model Compression
terms.definition: Techniques for reducing model size while maintaining performance, including pruning and distillation.

terms.name: Pruning
terms.definition: Removing less important weights or neurons from a neural network to reduce size and speed up inference.

terms.name: ONNX
terms.definition: Open format for representing ML models enabling interoperability between different frameworks.

terms.name: PyTorch
terms.definition: Facebook's deep learning framework widely used in research and increasingly in production.

terms.name: TensorFlow
terms.definition: Google's open-source deep learning framework for training and deploying ML models.

terms.name: Keras
terms.definition: High-level neural networks API built on top of TensorFlow for rapid prototyping.

terms.name: JAX
terms.definition: Google's numerical computing library with automatic differentiation and XLA compilation for ML research.

terms.name: Flax
terms.definition: Neural network library built on JAX used by Google for research on large-scale models.

terms.name: Hugging Face Spaces
terms.definition: Hosted environment for deploying and sharing ML model demos using Gradio or Streamlit.

terms.name: BentoML
terms.definition: Framework for packaging, deploying, and operating ML models as production services.

terms.name: Baseten
terms.definition: Platform for deploying and serving ML models with fast cold starts and GPU support.

terms.name: LLM Gateway
terms.definition: Proxy layer managing routing, caching, rate limiting, and observability for LLM API calls.

terms.name: Prompt Caching
terms.definition: Caching repeated prompt prefixes to reduce latency and cost for subsequent LLM API calls.

terms.name: Context Compression
terms.definition: Techniques for compressing conversation history or context to fit within a model's context window.

terms.name: Structured Output
terms.definition: Constraining LLM outputs to specific formats like JSON or XML for reliable downstream processing.

terms.name: Function Calling
terms.definition: LLM feature allowing models to call predefined functions with structured arguments.

terms.name: Agentic Loop
terms.definition: Repeated cycle of LLM reasoning, tool use, observation, and planning until a goal is achieved.

terms.name: ReAct
terms.definition: Prompting framework combining reasoning traces and actions for building reliable AI agents.

terms.name: Apify
terms.definition: Web scraping and automation platform for extracting data from websites at scale.

terms.name: Web Scraping
terms.definition: Automated extraction of data from websites for training datasets or knowledge bases.

terms.name: Synthetic Data
terms.definition: AI-generated data used for training when real data is scarce, private, or expensive to collect.

terms.name: Data Augmentation
terms.definition: Creating additional training examples by applying transformations to existing data.

terms.name: Active Learning
terms.definition: ML paradigm where the model selectively queries for labels on the most informative examples.

terms.name: Semi-supervised Learning
terms.definition: Training with a small amount of labeled data and a large amount of unlabeled data.

terms.name: Zero-shot Transfer
terms.definition: Applying a model to tasks it was never explicitly trained on using general knowledge.

terms.name: Adapter
terms.definition: Small trainable modules inserted into a pre-trained model for efficient task-specific fine-tuning.

terms.name: Attention Head
terms.definition: Individual parallel attention mechanism in multi-head attention; each head learns different patterns.

terms.name: Positional Encoding
terms.definition: Adding position information to token embeddings so transformers understand sequence order.

terms.name: RoPE
terms.definition: Relative positional encoding used in Llama and other modern LLMs for better length generalization.

terms.name: FlashAttention
terms.definition: IO-aware exact attention algorithm that significantly reduces memory usage and speeds up training.

terms.name: GQA
terms.definition: Attention variant grouping queries to reduce KV cache size and speed up inference in large models.

terms.name: MLA
terms.definition: DeepSeek's efficient attention mechanism compressing KV cache into low-rank latent vectors.

terms.name: SSM
terms.definition: Alternative to transformers using state space equations for efficient long-sequence modeling.

terms.name: Mamba
terms.definition: State space model architecture offering linear-time sequence modeling as an alternative to transformers.

terms.name: Sequence Modeling
terms.definition: Modeling and generating sequential data like text, audio, or time series.

terms.name: Prompt Injection
terms.definition: Attack where malicious input overrides an LLM's original instructions to manipulate its behavior.

terms.name: LLM Security
terms.definition: Practices and techniques for securing LLM applications against attacks, misuse, and data leakage.

terms.name: Jailbreaking
terms.definition: Techniques used to bypass an LLM's safety guardrails to generate prohibited content.

terms.name: Guardrails
terms.definition: Safety mechanisms built into LLMs or their deployment to prevent harmful outputs.

terms.name: AI Regulation
terms.definition: Laws and government policies governing the development and deployment of AI systems.

terms.name: EU AI Act
terms.definition: European Union's comprehensive regulation classifying and governing AI systems by risk level.

terms.name: Copilot Studio
terms.definition: Microsoft's platform for building custom AI copilots and chatbots with no-code and low-code tools.

terms.name: Chatbot
terms.definition: Conversational AI application that simulates dialogue with users through text or voice.

terms.name: RAG Pipeline
terms.definition: End-to-end system combining document ingestion, embedding, retrieval, and LLM generation.

terms.name: Hybrid Search
terms.definition: Combining keyword (BM25) and semantic vector search for more accurate retrieval.

terms.name: BM25
terms.definition: Classic probabilistic relevance function for keyword-based document retrieval.

terms.name: Evaluation
terms.definition: Process of measuring LLM quality using automated metrics, human ratings, or model-based judging.

terms.name: RAGAS
terms.definition: Framework for automated evaluation of RAG pipeline quality across faithfulness, relevance, and recall.

terms.name: LLM Judge
terms.definition: Using a strong LLM to evaluate the outputs of another LLM as a scalable evaluation technique.

terms.name: Evals
terms.definition: OpenAI's framework for evaluating LLM behavior; also general term for model evaluation suites.

terms.name: LangSmith
terms.definition: LangChain's observability and evaluation platform for debugging and monitoring LLM applications.

terms.name: Tracing
terms.definition: Recording the full execution trace of LLM calls, tool invocations, and agent steps for debugging.

terms.name: Observability
terms.definition: Monitoring, logging, and tracing AI systems in production to understand behavior and catch issues.

terms.name: Cost Optimization
terms.definition: Techniques for reducing LLM API costs including caching, smaller models, and batching.

terms.name: Inference Server
terms.definition: Dedicated server optimized for serving ML model predictions at scale with low latency.

terms.name: TensorRT
terms.definition: NVIDIA's SDK for high-performance deep learning inference with GPU optimization.

terms.name: NVIDIA
terms.definition: Leading GPU manufacturer whose hardware powers most AI training and inference workloads globally.

terms.name: H100
terms.definition: NVIDIA's flagship data center GPU for AI training, with 80GB HBM3 memory and transformer engine.

terms.name: Colab
terms.definition: Free cloud-based Jupyter notebook environment with GPU/TPU access for ML experimentation.

terms.name: Jupyter Notebook
terms.definition: Interactive computing environment for running code, visualizing data, and documenting ML experiments.

terms.name: Python
terms.definition: Dominant programming language for AI and ML due to its rich ecosystem of libraries.

terms.name: JavaScript
terms.definition: Primary language for frontend AI applications and increasingly used for backend AI with Node.js.

terms.name: TypeScript
terms.definition: Typed superset of JavaScript used extensively in modern AI web applications.

terms.name: Rust
terms.definition: Systems programming language increasingly used for building high-performance AI inference engines.

terms.name: Candle
terms.definition: Minimalist ML framework in Rust by Hugging Face for fast and lightweight inference.

terms.name: LiteLLM
terms.definition: Lightweight Python library to call any LLM using the OpenAI API format.

terms.name: DSPy
terms.definition: Stanford framework for programming language models by compiling declarative modules into optimized prompts.

terms.name: Instructor
terms.definition: Python library for structured outputs from LLMs using Pydantic validation.

terms.name: Pydantic
terms.definition: Python data validation library widely used for structured LLM outputs and API request/response schemas.

terms.name: CrewAI
terms.definition: Framework for building multi-agent AI systems with role-based agents collaborating on tasks.

terms.name: AutoGen
terms.definition: Microsoft's framework for building multi-agent conversational AI applications.

terms.name: Semantic Kernel
terms.definition: Microsoft's SDK for integrating LLMs into applications with plugins and memory.

terms.name: Haystack
terms.definition: Open-source framework for building search and RAG pipelines with multiple LLM backends.

terms.name: Cursor Rules
terms.definition: Custom instruction files for Cursor AI editor defining coding standards and project context.

terms.name: Claude.md
terms.definition: Anthropic's convention for providing Claude with project-level context and instructions in a markdown file.

terms.name: Tavily
terms.definition: Search API optimized for LLM agents providing clean, structured web search results.

terms.name: Firecrawl
terms.definition: Web crawling API that converts entire websites into clean markdown for LLM ingestion.

terms.name: Exa
terms.definition: Neural search engine designed for AI agents with embedding-based web search.

terms.name: Together AI
terms.definition: Platform for running and fine-tuning open-source LLMs on dedicated GPU clusters.

terms.name: Fireworks AI
terms.definition: Fast inference platform for open-source LLMs with compound AI system support.

terms.name: Cerebras
terms.definition: AI chip company with wafer-scale processor delivering the fastest LLM inference speeds.

terms.name: SambaNova
terms.definition: Enterprise AI hardware and software company offering fast inference for large models.

terms.name: Hugging Face Hub
terms.definition: Central repository hosting over 900,000 models, 200,000 datasets, and 300,000 demo spaces.

terms.name: Transformers Library
terms.definition: Open-source library providing thousands of pre-trained transformer models for NLP, vision, and audio.

terms.name: Diffusers
terms.definition: Library for state-of-the-art diffusion models for image, video, and audio generation.

terms.name: PEFT Library
terms.definition: Library implementing parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods like LoRA, LoHa, and prefix tuning.

terms.name: TRL
terms.definition: Hugging Face library for training LLMs with RLHF, PPO, and DPO algorithms.

terms.name: DPO
terms.definition: Alternative to RLHF that directly fine-tunes LLMs from human preference data without a reward model.

terms.name: Reward Model
terms.definition: Model trained on human preference data to score LLM outputs for use in RLHF training.

terms.name: SFT
terms.definition: First stage of LLM alignment training on high-quality instruction-response pairs.

terms.name: Instruction Tuning
terms.definition: Fine-tuning LLMs on instruction-following datasets to improve task generalization.

terms.name: Prefix Tuning
terms.definition: PEFT method prepending trainable tokens to the input instead of updating model weights.

terms.name: Prompt Tuning
terms.definition: Learning soft prompt embeddings prepended to inputs while keeping model weights frozen.

terms.name: Unsloth
terms.definition: Library enabling 2x faster LLM fine-tuning with 70% less VRAM through manual backprop optimizations.

terms.name: Axolotl
terms.definition: Popular open-source tool for fine-tuning LLMs supporting LoRA, QLoRA, and full fine-tuning.

terms.name: LM Studio
terms.definition: Desktop application for discovering, downloading, and running LLMs locally on your computer.

terms.name: Local LLM
terms.definition: Running LLMs on your own hardware instead of cloud APIs for privacy and cost savings.

terms.name: Open WebUI
terms.definition: Self-hosted ChatGPT-like interface for running local LLMs via Ollama.

terms.name: AnythingLLM
terms.definition: All-in-one desktop app for building private RAG chatbots with local or cloud LLMs.

terms.name: PrivateGPT
terms.definition: Open-source project for private document interaction using local LLMs with no data leaving your machine.

terms.name: Generative AI
terms.definition: AI systems that can generate new content including text, images, audio, video, and code.

terms.name: AI in Healthcare
terms.definition: Applications of AI in medical diagnosis, drug discovery, patient monitoring, and clinical workflows.

terms.name: Drug Discovery
terms.definition: Using AI to accelerate identification of drug candidates and predict molecular properties.

terms.name: Protein Folding
terms.definition: Using AI like AlphaFold to predict 3D protein structures from amino acid sequences.

terms.name: AlphaFold
terms.definition: DeepMind's AI system that predicts protein 3D structures with near-experimental accuracy.

terms.name: DeepMind
terms.definition: Google's AI research lab behind AlphaFold, AlphaGo, Gemini, and other landmark AI systems.

terms.name: AlphaGo
terms.definition: DeepMind's RL system that defeated world champions at the board game Go.

terms.name: Meta AI
terms.definition: Meta's AI research and product division behind Llama, CodeLlama, FAISS, and PyTorch.

terms.name: xAI
terms.definition: Elon Musk's AI company developing the Grok LLM with real-time X/Twitter data access.

terms.name: Cohere Command
terms.definition: Cohere's enterprise-grade LLM for generation, summarization, and instruction following.

terms.name: Gemma
terms.definition: Google's family of open-weight lightweight models based on Gemini research.

terms.name: Qwen
terms.definition: Alibaba's open-weight LLM family with strong multilingual and coding capabilities.

terms.name: Yi
terms.definition: Kai-Fu Lee's 01.AI company's open-weight bilingual LLM family.

terms.name: Sora
terms.definition: OpenAI's text-to-video generation model capable of creating realistic high-quality videos.

terms.name: Kling
terms.definition: Kuaishou's text-to-video AI model competing with Sora for high-quality video generation.

terms.name: Video AI
terms.definition: AI models capable of generating, editing, or extending video from text or image prompts.

terms.name: Runway ML
terms.definition: Creative AI platform for video generation and editing tools used in professional media production.

terms.name: Midjourney
terms.definition: AI image generation service known for high-quality artistic image creation via Discord.

terms.name: ControlNet
terms.definition: Extension for Stable Diffusion adding spatial control over image generation using conditioning inputs.

terms.name: ComfyUI
terms.definition: Node-based GUI for building Stable Diffusion workflows with fine-grained control.

terms.name: Segment Anything
terms.definition: Meta's foundation model for zero-shot image segmentation from any prompt.

terms.name: Grounding DINO
terms.definition: Open-set object detection model that detects objects from arbitrary text descriptions.

terms.name: LLaVA
terms.definition: Open-source multimodal instruction-tuned model connecting a vision encoder with Llama.

terms.name: Phi-3
terms.definition: Microsoft's small language model achieving strong performance through high-quality training data.

terms.name: Gemini Flash
terms.definition: Google's fast, cost-efficient Gemini model optimized for high-frequency tasks.

terms.name: Claude Haiku
terms.definition: Anthropic's fastest and most compact Claude model optimized for speed and cost efficiency.

terms.name: Claude Sonnet
terms.definition: Anthropic's balanced Claude model offering strong performance with good speed and cost.

terms.name: Claude Opus
terms.definition: Anthropic's most capable Claude model for complex reasoning and nuanced tasks.

terms.name: GPT-4o
terms.definition: OpenAI's omni model handling text, images, and audio natively in a single unified model.

terms.name: Llama 3
terms.definition: Meta's third generation open-weight LLM family with significantly improved capabilities.

terms.name: Mixtral
terms.definition: Mistral's Mixture of Experts model offering strong performance at efficient inference costs.

terms.name: Perplexity Labs
terms.definition: Perplexity AI's experimental models with online search capability for developers.

terms.name: AI Gateway
terms.definition: Unified API layer for routing, caching, and observing requests across multiple LLM providers.

terms.name: Portkey
terms.definition: AI gateway with observability, caching, fallbacks, and load balancing across LLM providers.

terms.name: Helicone
terms.definition: Open-source LLM observability platform for logging, monitoring, and debugging LLM requests.

terms.name: Braintrust
terms.definition: Enterprise AI evaluation and observability platform for testing and improving LLM applications.

terms.name: Weights
terms.definition: The learned parameters of a neural network stored as numerical matrices representing what the model knows.

terms.name: Parameters
terms.definition: The total count of learnable values in a neural network; larger models have more parameters.

terms.name: VRAM
terms.definition: GPU memory used to store model weights, activations, and KV cache during inference and training.

terms.name: HBM
terms.definition: High-speed GPU memory type used in data center GPUs like H100 for fast data transfer.

terms.name: Lora Rank
terms.definition: Hyperparameter controlling the size of low-rank adapter matrices in LoRA fine-tuning.

terms.name: Perplexity Score
terms.definition: Quantitative measure of how well a language model predicts a text sample.

terms.name: BLEU
terms.definition: Metric for evaluating machine translation quality by comparing to reference translations.

terms.name: ROUGE
terms.definition: Set of metrics for evaluating automatic summarization and translation quality.

terms.name: F1 Score
terms.definition: Harmonic mean of precision and recall; balanced metric for classification tasks.

terms.name: Precision
terms.definition: Fraction of positive predictions that are actually correct.

terms.name: Recall
terms.definition: Fraction of actual positives that were correctly identified by the model.

terms.name: AUC-ROC
terms.definition: Area under the receiver operating characteristic curve measuring classifier performance across thresholds.

terms.name: Cross Entropy
terms.definition: Standard loss function for classification and language modeling tasks.

terms.name: Softmax
terms.definition: Function converting raw scores into probability distribution over classes or tokens.

terms.name: Layer Norm
terms.definition: Normalization technique applied across features within a single sample, used in transformers.

terms.name: Residual Connection
terms.definition: Skip connections in neural networks adding layer input to output, enabling training of very deep networks.

terms.name: ResNet
terms.definition: Deep residual network architecture by Microsoft that enabled training of very deep CNNs.

terms.name: ViT
terms.definition: Applying transformer architecture directly to image patches for visual recognition tasks.

terms.name: RWKV
terms.definition: Hybrid RNN-transformer architecture combining transformer parallelism with RNN efficiency.
